target.name: Astera Labs
target.founder: Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, Casey Morrison

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster

data.narrative:
Astera Labs is a public-stage semiconductor company founded by Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, and Casey Morrison, having raised $713M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage public companies in Chips & Semiconductors: Cerebras ($23.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given the absence of disclosed revenue for Astera Labs, which precludes P/S multiple anchoring. The median valuation across the 4 public semiconductor peers is $3.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in market cap and commercialization stage within the public semiconductor cohort.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $3.7B, centered on the peer median of $3.2B. Key uncertainties include the material variance in peer valuations—a 23x spread between floor and ceiling—which signals limited precision in the peer-median method alone. The absence of revenue-based multiples and limited applicability of alternative triangulation methods constrain confidence in the range. Any revised valuation would benefit from disclosed financial performance or additional transaction comps within the semiconductor frontier-tech subset.
